mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint movement of rising industrial production and declining real personal savings indicates a supply-constrained economy where demand outpaces capacity, leading to profit margin expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds 108 and RSAFS falls below 550000
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, a sustained month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate acts as a pivot signal that reduces discount rate pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive during identified tightening-to-easing pivots, or the signal generates a negative Rank IC over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic momentum that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in months with payroll growth > 100k are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When real personal savings fall below 500,000 million dollars, consumer spending power is constrained, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the savings threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in industrial production and a decrease in the federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting supply chain normalization, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL changes.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a sharp decline in the unemployment rate coupled with rising housing starts signals a broad-based economic rebound that fuels equity risk appetite and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when both the unemployment drop and housing surge occur, or the signal generates a negative Rank IC over a 1-year rolling window.
